levels.name: Initial
levels.description: Ad-hoc and reactive. No formal processes. Issues addressed as they arise.

levels.name: Managed
levels.description: Basic processes exist but are inconsistently applied. Dependent on individuals.

levels.name: Quantitative
levels.description: Data-driven. KPIs tracked. Management uses metrics to drive decisions.

levels.name: Optimising
levels.description: Continuous improvement culture. Predictive analytics. Proactive excellence.

measures.name: ICT Governance - Do we know what we need to do?
measures.description: Tracks whether Roles and Responsibilities are formally defined, owned and consistently performed. Without this foundation, risk cannot be managed and reporting has nothing to measure.

levels.label: Initial — No Roles and Responsibilities defined for this capability. Ownership is absent and activities are performed ad-hoc. Nobody is accountable if something goes wrong.
levels.exit: Define and document the most important Roles and Responsibilities for this capability. Assign at least one named owner who accepts accountability for ensuring those responsibilities are performed.

levels.label: Managed — Roles and Responsibilities have been drafted and a named owner assigned. The document exists but has not been formally approved and is not yet consistently followed in practice.
levels.exit: Obtain formal approval of the Roles and Responsibilities document from the appropriate authority. Confirm the owner accepts accountability and begins actively performing and enforcing their responsibilities.

levels.label: Defined — Roles and Responsibilities are formally approved and consistently performed. The owner is accountable and the organisation can demonstrate that responsibilities are being carried out.
levels.exit: Establish a formal review cycle for the Roles and Responsibilities document. Complete at least one review that considers inputs from the ICT Risk and Reporting dimensions — demonstrating the feedback loop has begun.

levels.label: Quantitative — Roles and Responsibilities are reviewed on a regular schedule. Each review considers risk findings and KPI outcomes and uses them to validate or update who is responsible and how.
levels.exit: Demonstrate that at least one Risk finding or KPI gap has directly resulted in a documented update to the Roles and Responsibilities — providing evidence that the continuous improvement loop is operating.

levels.label: Optimising — Roles and Responsibilities are continuously updated in response to Risk findings and KPI outcomes. The improvement loop is fully closed, embedded in practice and demonstrably driving better outcomes.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous review and demonstrate ongoing responsiveness to inputs from the ICT Risk and Reporting dimensions.

measures.name: ICT Risk - What happens if we don't do what we need we do?
measures.description: Tracks risk and control coverage against our Roles and Responsibilities, and whether gaps and findings feed back to update the Governance Profile — closing the improvement loop.

levels.label: Initial — No risks or controls have been identified against any Role or Responsibility for this capability. We do not yet know what could go wrong if our responsibilities are not performed.
levels.exit: Identify the most important Roles and Responsibilities for this capability. For each one, draft at least one risk describing what could go wrong if that responsibility is not performed, and at least one control to prevent or detect that failure.

levels.label: Managed — Risks and controls have been drafted against the most important Roles and Responsibilities. We know what could go wrong but have not yet formally validated whether our controls are adequate or assessed the residual risk.
levels.exit: Formally assess every drafted risk and control. For each risk, document the residual risk score after controls are applied. Identify Roles and Responsibilities where controls are missing, untested or ineffective — these are the gaps to treat.

levels.label: Defined — All drafted risks and controls have been formally assessed and approved. Risks and controls are documented in Riskonnect and included in the RCSA and CSA cycle. We have a clear and agreed picture of where we stand.
levels.exit: For every gap identified — missing controls, ineffective controls or unacceptable residual risk — define and implement a treatment action. Reassess the residual risk after each treatment is applied and document the updated position.

levels.label: Quantitative — Treatment actions have been implemented and residual risks reassessed. The risk profile for this capability is formally established, actively tracked and reported. We can demonstrate the impact of our risk management work.
levels.exit: Confirm that all residual risks are within the defined tolerance. Establish a formal reassessment cycle and complete at least one full cycle — then demonstrate that risk findings are being fed back to the Governance Profile to update Roles and Responsibilities.

levels.label: Optimising — All risks are within tolerance and the reassessment cycle is continuous and active. Risk and control findings proactively drive updates to the Governance Profile — the improvement loop is fully closed and self-sustaining.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous reassessment and demonstrate that risk findings are actively and consistently driving updates to the Governance Profile.

measures.name: ICT Reporting - Is what we doing working well?
measures.description: Tracks whether KPIs are grounded in our Roles and Responsibilities and risks, reported to management on schedule, and whether gaps trigger updates to the Governance Profile — completing the continuous improvement cycle.

levels.label: Initial — No KPIs defined for this capability. There is no visibility of whether Roles and Responsibilities are being performed, whether controls are working, or whether outcomes are being achieved.
levels.exit: Define at least one KPI for this capability that is directly grounded in the Roles and Responsibilities and the identified risks. The KPI must measure something that tells you whether a responsibility is being performed or a control is working. Assign an owner responsible for measuring and reporting it.

levels.label: Managed — KPIs have been defined and are grounded in the Roles and Responsibilities and identified risks. They are not yet being measured consistently or reported to stakeholders.
levels.exit: Begin measuring all defined KPIs on a regular schedule. Produce at least one formal report showing KPI results and distribute it to relevant stakeholders — demonstrating that measurement is active and not just planned.

levels.label: Defined — KPIs are measured and reported periodically. Results reach the right stakeholders and are reviewed. There is now visibility of performance but gaps and exceptions are not yet formally escalated or acted upon.
levels.exit: Establish a formal reporting schedule and define escalation thresholds. When a KPI falls below an agreed level, a formal exception must be raised, tracked and assigned to an owner — creating accountability for the gap.

levels.label: Quantitative — KPIs are reported to management on a formal schedule. Exceptions are escalated, tracked and owned. Reporting is reliable, consistent and used to hold accountable those responsible for performance.
levels.exit: Demonstrate that at least one KPI gap or formal exception has directly triggered a review of the Governance Roles and Responsibilities document — providing evidence that reporting outcomes are feeding back into the improvement loop.

levels.label: Optimising — KPI trends are analysed proactively and gaps consistently drive updates to the Governance Profile. Reporting is no longer just measurement — it is the mechanism by which continuous improvement is sustained.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous KPI review and demonstrate ongoing responsiveness — that reporting outcomes are actively and consistently driving updates to Roles and Responsibilities.

capabilities.name: ICT Asset Management
capabilities.description: Lifecycle management of ICT hardware, software, and cloud assets from procurement through to decommissioning.

capabilities.name: ICT Backup, Response & Recovery Management
capabilities.description: Plans, procedures and capabilities to recover ICT services following a major disruption or disaster.

capabilities.name: ICT Capacity & Performance Management
capabilities.description: Monitoring and planning of ICT resource performance and capacity to meet current and future demands.

capabilities.name: ICT Change Management
capabilities.description: Controlled management of changes to ICT services and infrastructure to minimise risk.

capabilities.name: ICT Data Management
capabilities.description: Management of data quality, classification, storage, retention, and governance to ensure data is accurate, secure, and accessible.

capabilities.name: ICT Governance Management
capabilities.description: Frameworks, policies, and oversight mechanisms that ensure ICT activities align with organisational strategy and compliance obligations.

capabilities.name: ICT Incident Management
capabilities.description: Restoration of normal service operation as quickly as possible following unplanned disruptions.

capabilities.name: ICT Major Incident Management
capabilities.description: Coordinated response to high-impact ICT incidents to minimise business disruption and enable rapid service restoration.

capabilities.name: ICT Third-Party Risk Management
capabilities.description: Assessment and ongoing management of risks introduced by third-party vendors, suppliers, and managed service providers.

capabilities.name: ICT Systems Acquisition, Development & Maintenance
capabilities.description: Governance of the secure acquisition, development, testing, and ongoing maintenance of ICT systems and applications.

capabilities.name: ICT Vulnerability Management
capabilities.description: Identification, assessment, and remediation of security vulnerabilities across ICT infrastructure.
